com.nexacro.java.xapi.tx

Class PlatformHttpClient

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.nexacro.java.xapi.tx.PlatformHttpClient

  • public class PlatformHttpClient
extends java.lang.Object

    HTTP communication; transmit/receive data(PlatformData).

    below is a data transmission/reception example using PlatformHttpClient. 

     PlatformData reqData = ...;

 String url = "http://host/context/service.jsp";
 PlatformHttpClient client = new PlatformHttpClient(url, PlatformType.CONTENT_TYPE_XML);

 client.sendData(reqData);

 PlatformData resData = client.receiveData();

 client.close();
    Author:
    Choi, Jongkwan

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description

      URL location possessing creator.

      URL location and transmission/reception type(contentType) possessing creator.
      PlatformHttpClient(java.lang.String spec, java.lang.String contentType, java.lang.String charset)
      URL location, transmission/reception type(contentType) and character set(charset) possessing creator.

      When exception occurs, inspect received data(stream) save status.

      If isStreamLogEnabled is "true", set maximum buffer size to save receiving data(stream).

      • getContentType

        Return transmission/reception type(contentType).

      • getProtocolType

        public java.lang.String getProtocolType(int index)
        Return protocol type(protocolType). protocol type indicates data transformation type as compression and encryption.
        Parameters:
        index - location
        See Also:
        PlatformType.PROTOCOL_TYPE_ZLIB

      • addProtocolType

        public void addProtocolType(java.lang.String protocolType)
        Add protocol type(protocolType). protocol type indicates data transformation type as compression and encryption.
        Parameters:
        protocolType - protocol type
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - When adding repetition of protocol type
        See Also:
        PlatformType.PROTOCOL_TYPE_ZLIB

      • removeProtocolType

        public void removeProtocolType(int index)
        delete protocol type(protocolType).
        Parameters:
        index - location

      • removeProtocolType

        public void removeProtocolType(java.lang.String protocolType)
        Delete protocol type(protocolType).
        Parameters:
        protocolType - protocol type

        public boolean containsProtocolType(java.lang.String protocolType)
        Inspect protocol type(protocolType) inclusion status.
        Parameters:
        protocolType - protocol type
        Returns:
        protocol type inclusion status

      • clearProtocolTypes

        public void clearProtocolTypes()
        Delete all protocol type(protocolType).

      • getProtocolTypeCount

        public int getProtocolTypeCount()
        Return protocol type(protocolType) count.
        Returns:
        protocol type count

      • addRequestHeader

        public void addRequestHeader(java.lang.String key,
                             java.lang.String value)
        Adds a transmission header.
        Parameters:
        key - key of header
        value - value of header

      • isVerbose

        public boolean isVerbose()
        Inspect INFO log output status. Basic value is "false", and set to output logging component INFO log, and log is outputted only when verbose is "true".
        Returns:
        log output status
        See Also:
        setVerbose(boolean)

      • setVerbose

        public void setVerbose(boolean verbose)
        Set INFO log output status.
        Parameters:
        verbose - INFO log output status
        See Also:
        isVerbose()

      • isStreamLogEnabled

        public boolean isStreamLogEnabled()
        When exception occurs, inspect received data(stream) save status. For Debug usage-using much memory; only use when necessary.
        Returns:
        save status
        See Also:
        setStreamLogEnabled(boolean), getStreamLogDir()

      • setStreamLogEnabled

        public void setStreamLogEnabled(boolean isStreamLogEnabled)
        When exception occurs, inspect received data(stream) save status. If received data(stream) saving is desired, it must be set. For Debug usage-using much memory; only use when necessary.
        Parameters:
        isStreamLogEnabled - save status
        See Also:
        isStreamLogEnabled(), getStreamLogDir()

      • getStreamLogDir

        public java.lang.String getStreamLogDir()
        When exception occurs, returns location to save received data(stream). For Debug usage-using much memory; only use when necessary.
        Returns:
        saving location
        See Also:
        isStreamLogEnabled(), setStreamLogDir(String)

      • setStreamLogDir

        public void setStreamLogDir(java.lang.String streamLogDir)
        When exception occurs, returns location to save received data(stream). If received data(stream) saving is desired, it must be set. For Debug usage-using much memory; only use when necessary.
        Parameters:
        streamLogDir - saving location
        See Also:
        isStreamLogEnabled(), getStreamLogDir()

      • getStreamLogMaxSize

        public int getStreamLogMaxSize()
        If isStreamLogEnabled is "true", return the maximum buffer size to save received data(stream). Basic value is 4MB. For Debug usage-using much memory; only use when necessary.
        Returns:
        maximum buffer size
        See Also:
        isStreamLogEnabled(), setStreamLogMaxSize(int)

      • setStreamLogMaxSize

        public void setStreamLogMaxSize(int streamLogMaxSize)
        If isStreamLogEnabled is "true", set maximum buffer size to save receiving data(stream). For Debug usage-using much memory; only use when necessary.
        Parameters:
        streamLogMaxSize - maximum buffer size
        See Also:
        isStreamLogEnabled(), getStreamLogMaxSize()

      • sendData

        public void sendData(PlatformData data)
              throws PlatformException
        Transmit data(PlatformData). If transmission/reception type(contentType) is set, PlatformType.DEFAULT_CONTENT_TYPE is applied.
        Throws:
        PlatformException

      • receiveData

        public PlatformData receiveData()
                         throws PlatformException
        Receive data(PlatformData). If transmission/reception type(contentType) is set, PlatformType.DEFAULT_CONTENT_TYPE is applied.
        Throws:
        PlatformException

      • close

        public void close()
        Close HTTP connection.
